prompt_queue.prompt: Bootstrap the repository's technical, developer-facing living documentation from source code. Treat this as a first-run documentation bootstrap. Study README.md, docs/**, source directories, tests, examples, issues, and pull requests, then create a clean documentation surface that covers every major public API, extension point, runtime contract, workflow/lifecycle path, data contract, storage/persistence boundary, auth/permission behavior, failure mode, operational workflow, and contributor-facing design principle. Organize the output into a concise docs index plus separate digestible topic pages whose hierarchy mirrors the repository architecture and logical module boundaries. Use clear titles, bounded scope, parent/child relationships, and useful cross-links so readers can move from architecture overview to focused implementation details without reading a monolithic page. Write every linked documentation page before opening a pull request, and verify the final index and page links resolve to committed files. Prefer documentation that readers can navigate and understand quickly over audit artifacts. Use create_or_update_github_file before create_github_pull_request. If tool, token, or writable-scope limits constrain the pass, state the partial coverage in the committed docs and pull request body. Use no_changes when existing docs already provide a complete, navigable technical docs surface across the full codebase.
